name: TC-SF-008 — POST /api/v1/fetch/trigger: No auth must return 401
description:
**What this test does:**
Posts to /spr-fetcher/api/v1/fetch/trigger without any Authorization header and verifies the server responds with 401 or 403.

**Why it matters:** The trigger endpoint initiates a data fetch from the KCB API. If it does not require authentication, any external actor could initiate an unlimited number of fetch operations, consuming KCB API quota and causing excessive load on both the spr-fetcher service and the KCB API.

**Note:** Due to BUG-SF-002, the endpoint may also return a connection-level error for unauthenticated requests. If that occurs, the test reports the connection error and fails, as the auth enforcement cannot be verified.

What this test does:
Posts to /spr-fetcher/api/v1/fetch/trigger without any Authorization header and verifies the server responds with 401 or 403.

Why it matters: The trigger endpoint initiates a data fetch from the KCB API. If it does not require authentication, any external actor could initiate an unlimited number of fetch operations, consuming KCB API quota and causing excessive load on both the spr-fetcher service and the KCB API.

Note: Due to BUG-SF-002, the endpoint may also return a connection-level error for unauthenticated requests. If that occurs, the test reports the connection error and fails, as the auth enforcement cannot be verified.

Error: No-auth trigger must return 401/403, got 504

expect(received).toContain(expected) // indexOf

Expected value: 504
Received array: [401, 403]
